commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `src/gateway/session-transcript-readers.ts` and routes selected gateway, agent, status, and TUI transcript read/tail/count/title/message lookup callers through scoped facade calls while preserving file-backed storage.

PR surface: Source +497, Tests +197. Total +694 across 32 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a behavior-neutral refactor PR rather than a bug report. The relevant check is source/test proof that current file-backed transcript reads are preserved through the new scoped facade.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining action is human owner review of a draft, protected, stacked session-state refactor rather than a narrow automated repair.

target.title: refactor: add transcript reader seam
target.body:
## What

Path 3 / PR 3.1b transcript readers: add a storage-agnostic transcript reader facade and route storage-neutral transcript read/tail/stats/message lookup/projection callers through it. Refs #88838.

## Why

The session accessor seam from PR 3.1a gives the SQLite decomposition a clean branch-by-abstraction point, but transcript readers still needed a caller-facing seam before any SQLite-backed transcript store can replace the file-backed implementation. This keeps current file-backed behavior while removing direct reader coupling from gateway, agent, status, and TUI caller paths.

## Changes

- Add `src/gateway/session-transcript-readers.ts`
- Route gateway transcript readers
- Route agent/TUI/status readers
- Update reader mocks and tests
- Keep `session-utils.ts` compatibility exports
- Leave auto-reply/file-semantic paths unchanged

| Area | Change |
|------|--------|
| Gateway chat/history | Scoped reads for chat history, message lookup, SSE history, artifacts, image attachment indexing, reset hooks, and session events |
| Agent tools/runtime | Scoped reads for embedded gateway stub, sessions history/list tools, subagent output/recovery, and restart recovery |
| Compatibility | `session-utils.ts` reader re-exports now pass through the transcript reader facade |

Source branch commits:

- `cdeb1b4cc5` - `refactor: add transcript reader seam facade`
- `be70ff961e` - `refactor: route transcript readers through facade`

No storage flip, runtime dual-read/fallback, SQLite schema work, or SQLite runtime wiring is included. No current blockers.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-transcript-readers.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.fs.test.ts src/gateway/session-history-state.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-history-http.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-history-http.revocation.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/artifacts.test.ts src/agents/tools/embedded-gateway-stub.test.ts src/agents/subagent-announce-output.test.ts src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/sessions-list-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.sessions.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs ...`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...`
- `git diff --check`
- `autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD` clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- Disposable SQLite loop proof: `integration/sqlite-flip-transcript-readers` at `b09228b917`; `clawdbot-291` was resolved/backported before final proof

Behavior addressed: transcript reader callers now use the 3.1a reader seam while preserving file-backed behavior.
Real environment tested: local Codex worktree plus disposable SQLite validation branch.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused Vitest shards, `tsgo` core lanes, oxlint, oxfmt, diff check, autoreview, and the disposable SQLite loop proof listed above.
Evidence after fix: focused tests/type/lint/format/autoreview passed; integration proof branch `integration/sqlite-flip-transcript-readers` commit `b09228b917` passed after `clawdbot-291` backport.
Observed result after fix: no direct storage-neutral transcript reader caller remains on `session-utils.fs`; remaining direct imports are file-backed facade/test, archive cleanup, or active auto-reply-owned paths.
What was not tested: broad full-suite/Testbox beyond the disposable SQLite loop proof.
